mission.title: kubevirt: KubeVirt coding guidelines or best practices
mission.description: KubeVirt coding guidelines or best practices. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubevirt deployment
steps.description:
Verify your virtctl version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
helm list -n kubevirt 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevirt installation.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
KubeVirt could need a coding guideline or at least best practices for the codebase, to avoid code which is harder than necessary to understand and to maintain.

steps.title: Apply the fix for KubeVirt coding guidelines or best practices
steps.description:
Before this PR:
In vmi-create-admitter.go there are a lot places where we could return early and avoid unnecessary nesting.

After this PR:
Simplify the code nesting

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "KubeVirt coding guidelines or best practices" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Before this PR:
In vmi-create-admitter.go there are a lot places where we could return early and avoid unnecessary nesting.

After this PR:
Simplify the code nesting

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
